name: Florence Cathedral (Duomo)
overview: The Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore, commonly known as the Duomo, is Florence's iconic cathedral famous for its magnificent Renaissance dome designed by Filippo Brunelleschi. It's a masterpiece of Italian Gothic architecture and engineering.
history: Construction began in 1296 by Arnolfo di Cambio in the Gothic style and was completed structurally in 1436 with Brunelleschi's revolutionary dome. The façade was completed much later in the 19th century (1887) in the Gothic Revival style.
highlights: Brunelleschi's Dome - first dome built since antiquity without temporary wooden supports
463 steps to climb to the top of the dome for panoramic city views
Stunning façade of white, pink, and green marble in geometric patterns
Vasari and Zuccaro's frescoes of the Last Judgment inside the dome
tips: Book timed entry tickets online for dome climb. Dress modestly (covered shoulders and knees). Dome climb is strenuous and not recommended for those with claustrophobia or heart conditions. Best views from Piazzale Michelangelo at sunset.
